'''Haariden''' was a [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] and the [[lava crab]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. It was [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by a sole [[Moon/Legends|moon]], which appeared pink from the planet's surface.

Beginning in [[37 BBY/Legends|37 BBY]], when the potential for [[titanite]] mines was discovered, the planet was caught up in a civil war over land disputes and future mining rights. It was later discovered that the mineral could, in its synthesized form, produce a key component in [[bacta]]—but titanite was extremely rare and hard to mine, usually buried so deep near a planet's core that it almost always cost more to extract than it was ever worth.

The civil war ended in [[26 BBY/Legends|26 BBY]] when a team of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knights]] arrived on a [[Mission to Haariden|rescue mission]] to retrieve five [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] who had traveled there on an environmental research mission sponsored by the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. The scientists were [[Fort Turan]] (mission leader), [[Granta Omega|Tic Verdun]] (scout, [[Force void/Legends|Force Blank]] [[Granta Omega]] in disguise), [[Joveh D'a Alin]], [[Talie Heathe]], and [[Reug Yucon]].

[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] were assigned to the Jedi mission, along with [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Soara Antana/Legends|Soara Antana]] and her [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Darra Thel-Tanis]], who was wounded there. By mission's end, peace was restored to the planet when Omega's machinations against that peace were put asunder through the actions of Kenobi and Skywalker.

{{Planet-stub}}
